open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary, and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's new module duplicate-pages.js (TASK-351) is NOT a new ingest boundary — it reads the ALREADY-OPEN, already-validated document via doc.getData() and uploads nothing. It does, however, add a bounded client-side output-amplification path: with copies capped at MAX_COPIES=20 and all pages selected, output pages = numPages*(1+20) = numPages*21. This is the same LOW self-DoS family as extract-pages.js (uploader's own tab only, no server impact) and is bounded by the same single fix — the viewer-load page-count cap. No new boundary, no separate cap needed beyond that. All remaining gaps are client-side-only DoS on the 1.6 GiB box; no server-side risk. Single right developer fix remains: page-count cap once at viewer load (covers all per-page consumers) + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles().
